body.title: /autocompact runs compaction on Haiku (model haiku -> compact -> model sonnet)
body.result: /autocompact step 2 does model haiku -> compact -> model sonnet; levers doc updated; repo+userSettings synced
body.intention: I intend that /autocompact performs the compaction summarization on Haiku and returns the session to Sonnet afterward.
body.description:
Akien prefers Haiku for compaction — cheap, fast, and it works on standard context without 1M credits. /autocompact step 2 currently fires a bare /compact (which runs on the working model). Update it to do the model-switch dance: /model haiku -> /compact <preserve string> -> /model sonnet, via sequential tmux send-keys with delays long enough for the async /compact to finish before switching back. Keep the existing 3-Enter interrupt pattern before the command (T-autocompact-interruption-protocol).

**Affected files:** skills/autocompact/SKILL.md (repo root — canonical), ~/.claude/skills/autocompact/SKILL.md (sync copy)
**Design rules:** skills-in-repo (repo root is source of truth, sync to ~/.claude); no DB rules apply
**Scope boundary:** In scope: the model-switch sequence inside /autocompact step 2 and its tmux send-keys sequencing. Out of scope: WHEN /autocompact fires (that is T-cc-compact-cadence-hook); the value of N.
**Completion criteria:** /autocompact SKILL.md step 2 issues /model haiku, then /compact, then /model sonnet, with sequencing notes; both repo and ~/.claude copies match; levers doc docs/the_ownership_gap/4.Skills.md updated to describe the Haiku-dance behavior.
